topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sudden intrusion of the bouquet into my hands felt like a physical imposition. They were garish, an overabundance of wilting roses in shades of aggressive pink and cheap yellow. I stared at the stems, the plastic wrapping already beginning to crinkle under the pressure of my grip. There was no accompanying note, no flimsy excuse for this unsolicited display. My jaw tightened, the muscles in my neck corded as I forced myself to look at the arrangement. It was utterly superfluous. I tucked the bunch awkwardly under my arm, the petals brushing against my sleeve with an irritating softness, and made a pointed, almost imperceptible movement toward the nearest exit.

[story 2]

I watched the delivery person—a young man with an overly bright smile—retreat down the pavement. The flowers, heavy and undeniably cheerful, were now resting on the polished mahogany of my entryway table. I didn't touch them immediately. Instead, I circled the arrangement slowly, my gaze lingering on the imperfectly formed edges of the petals. A slow, deliberate exhale escaped my lips, a sound devoid of warmth. "What is this?" I murmured, the question laced with a flat, unimpressed tone that I hoped conveyed everything I felt. I picked up a nearby magazine, pretending intense interest in the glossy pages, while my eyes kept flicking back to the unsolicited gift.

[story 3]

The sheer audacity of it caught me off guard. A vibrant splash of color against the muted tones of my otherwise sterile apartment. I took the flowers, the stems cool and damp against my fingertips, as if they were contaminated. I examined the wrapping paper—a flimsy, glossy material that seemed entirely too eager to shout its message. I didn't bother to examine the blooms closely; they were already beginning to droop at the edges, a testament to poor planning. With a sharp, almost violent flick of my wrist, I tossed the entire bunch onto the counter, the rustle of the paper sounding like a small, irritating protest. I turned away, focusing intently on straightening a misplaced book, determined to erase the memory of the intrusion.
